description: Wan 2.2 14B image-to-video GENERATION with the lightx2v 4-step Lightning LoRAs. Two-expert (high-noise → low-noise) MoE: a high-noise KSamplerAdvanced runs the first 2 steps, a low-noise one finishes — each with its matching 4-step Lightning LoRA, so a full clip renders in 4 sampler steps. Graph: the official ComfyUI template 'video_wan2_2_14B_i2v', FLATTENED from its subgraph form by wan22.derive.py (comfy_run's UI→API converter is flat-graph only). Additive to wan_vace_inpaint — Wan 2.2 has no VACE-style masked inpainting, so VACE 2.1 stays the cleanplate/removal tool; this is the fast generator.

about.what: Turns a single starting image into a short video. Fast: it uses the 4-step 'Lightning' speed-ups, so a clip renders in seconds-to-a-minute of sampling rather than minutes.
about.inputs: One starting image, plus a written description of how the scene should move.
about.key_knobs: Prompt — describe the motion/action. Frames — clip length (16·n+1, 81 ≈ 5s at 16fps). Width/Height — output size; match your input image's shape.

_HOWITRUNS: No-build path (same as wan_vace_inpaint): a public ComfyUI base image runs comfy_run.py, which seeds ComfyUI from the bundle, downloads the 2 diffusion experts + 2 Lightning LoRAs + umt5 + vae onto the Spark node, converts this flat UI graph to API via /object_info, applies patches, and renders. All node types are CORE ComfyUI (no node_packs) — but recent ones (WanImageToVideo, CreateVideo, SaveVideo), so the base image must ship a current ComfyUI; the cu130 yanwk tag does. ~45 GB is fetched on a COLD start (use --idle-hold to keep a warm node). --convert-only skips the model download and validates the flattened graph cheaply.
_TWOEXPERT: The MoE split lives on the two KSamplerAdvanced nodes: 110 (high-noise) add_noise=enable, start_at_step 0, end_at_step 2, return_with_leftover_noise=enable; 111 (low-noise) add_noise=disable, start_at_step 2, end_at_step 4. Total 4 steps, cfg 1 (Lightning). To run the BASE model without Lightning: set both Lightning strengths to 0 and raise total steps (e.g. high 0→10, low 10→20) with cfg ~3.5 via --set on nodes 110/111.

primary_input.label: Start image (i2v)
primary_input.tooltip: The first frame. The video animates outward from this image per your prompt. Set Width/Height below to your image's aspect for the cleanest result.

prompt.default: The subject comes alive with natural, fluid motion; the camera holds steady. Cinematic lighting, crisp detail.

ui.label: Prompt
ui.tooltip: Describe how the scene MOVES (action, camera). Wan 2.2 has strong motion/prompt adherence. Note: the Lightning 4-step LoRAs trade some motion range for speed — for big dynamic motion, lower the Lightning strengths and raise steps (see _TWOEXPERT).

negative.default: static, still, frozen, blurry, low quality, jpeg artifacts, distorted, deformed, extra limbs, flicker, ghosting, watermark, text

ui.label: Negative prompt
ui.tooltip: Discourages stillness and common artifacts. (The official template ships a long Chinese quality string here; this is a clean English equivalent — either works.)

ui.label: Width
ui.tooltip: Wan 2.2 14B is a 480p–720p model; 1280x720 is the sweet spot. Divisible by 16. Match your start image's aspect. (src: Wan 2.2 docs)

ui.label: Height
ui.tooltip: 720p sweet spot (max dim ~1280); divisible by 16. Match the start image's aspect. (src: Wan 2.2 docs)

ui.label: Frames (length)
ui.tooltip: Wan frame count = 16·n+1 (81 ≈ 5s, 161 ≈ 10s) at 16 fps. (src: Wan-Video/Wan2.2)

ui.label: Lightning strength (high-noise)
ui.tooltip: Strength of the 4-step Lightning LoRA on the high-noise expert. 1.0 = full speed-up. Lower it (toward 0) for more motion range at the cost of more steps. Set 0 to disable Lightning (then raise steps on node 110/111).

ui.label: Lightning strength (low-noise)
ui.tooltip: Strength of the 4-step Lightning LoRA on the low-noise expert. Keep equal to the high-noise strength unless you're tuning.
